About me

The work

Selected Projects — 2024/26
01

Vision to Sketch

Observational Drawing · Interior Design

A comparison study of a cozy living room corner — captured and hand-sketched. The real-life inspiration features a neutral-toned sofa, textured cushions, and a minimalist arching lamp over a rustic side table. The hand-drawn sketch translates the scene into intricate pen strokes, capturing structure, texture, and the essence of the space.

02

Café Design — "Do you have a moment?"

Interior Design · Space Planning · Mood Board

A first design project challenging the brief of designing a café using Rayon. The concept — "Do you have a moment?" — places human connection at the heart of every decision. Long communal tables, intimate circular corners, an open kitchen, and thoughtful greenery create a space where strangers become friends and silence holds comfort.

03

4BHK Residential Design

Space Planning · Zoning · Schematic Design

A residential design project focused on creating a functional and comfortable 4BHK home based on client needs. The design emphasises efficient zoning, privacy, and smooth circulation — dividing spaces into public, private, and service zones across three floors, with schematic diagrams developed through iterative spatial thinking.

04

The Psychology of Space

UX Research · Survey · Spatial Psychology

A short survey conducted to understand how spatial environments influence human emotions — 10 responses, 3 key questions.

Do you think the design of a space affects your mood?

90% of people believe space directly affects their mood.

What kind of space makes you feel the most calm?

  • Bright & open — 40%
  • Cozy — 20%
  • Nature-inspired — 30%
  • Dark & enclosed — 10%

Which element impacts your mood the most in a space?

  • Lighting — 60%
  • Layout / Space planning — 30%
  • Furniture — 10%
Key Insights

Most people feel that space directly impacts their mood. Calm environments are usually minimal, open, and well-lit — while cluttered spaces tend to feel stressful. Lighting emerged as the single most influential element, followed by layout and space planning.

Expertise

What I do

01

UX/UI Exploration

Investigating how people interact with the world to design interfaces that feel genuinely intuitive — not just visually polished, but deeply considered.

02

Creative Thinking

Approaching every brief with curiosity and an open mind. Connecting unexpected ideas to arrive at solutions that are original, relevant, and memorable.

03

Visual Design Concepts

Translating ideas into visual languages with a strong sense of composition, colour, and detail — building identities and systems that speak before a word is read.

04

Thoughtful User Experiences

Designing end-to-end experiences shaped by empathy and emotion — so every interaction leaves people feeling seen, supported, and understood.
